content: OWL DL consistency reasoning (TBox: class satisfiability, subsumption, disjointness, property domain/range coherence) is an open-world, schema-level check normally run at ontology-authoring/design time, infrequently, over the whole ontology. SHACL validation (ABox/instance-level, closed-world) is run per data-change/per-instance and is the appropriate mechanism for 'does this data conform to expectations' checks at runtime. Practical combined pipelines (e.g. graphs using SHACL shapes derived from an OWL TBox) run OWL consistency checks in CI/unit-test style on schema changes, and run SHACL validation on every write/every validation pass against live data — mirroring exactly the DG split between rare TBox reasoning and frequent per-run SHACL instance checking.
